# SMART Goal Examples — Connect Planning Reference

> [!IMPORTANT]
> This file contains org-level uber goal titles and SMART goal examples used to populate the "Plan for the future" section of your Connect. **Update this file each fiscal year** with the current uber goal titles and new examples provided by your organization. The agent uses the goal titles here verbatim in the output artifacts.

---

## How to use this file

- Copy each goal title exactly into the "Plan for the future" section of your Connect.
- Review the examples and craft 2–4 SMART goals specific to your area and level.
- Think in headlines: what impact will you be able to describe 6 months from now?
- Go beyond routine tasks ("I addressed UUF items") — describe the strategic outcome.
- Include at least one AI-related goal per section per org guidance.

---

## Goal 1: Deliver secure, high-quality content for customers

We are our customers' trusted way to learn about Microsoft technologies. As content developers, we deliver accurate, high-quality, and discoverable content ready for human and agentic consumption. That content helps customers solve problems, make informed decisions, and build confidently. We collaborate with subject matter experts across product and partner teams to ensure our content reflects product truth and customer signals. AI is embedded in our day-to-day workflows, enabling us to scale our efforts and maintain quality more efficiently than ever before. Throughout the content lifecycle, we uphold rigorous standards for quality and trustworthiness.

### SMART goal examples

- By April, improve the AI readiness and quality score of my content by at least 6 percentage points each (current baseline is X for AI readiness and Y for quality) to get closer to the minimum target of 85% set by the content team by acting as the first customer for my assigned area and ensuring my top 60 PV articles are free of customer reported defects, are aligned with approved patterns, and have been fully refreshed for accuracy.
- By April, I will use human-validated AI research to identify the top 5 high-impact customer scenarios identified in conjunction with the product team, and create a minimum of 2 targeted articles per sprint that address these pain points and reduce support call volume by >5% (current baseline is: X).
- By January, I will host a doc bash with Engineering to review the top 20 nonfresh articles for accuracy, resulting in a 2% increase in the XYZ service score as reported in the Content Quality Report.
- Improve the quality score of AKS Fleet articles from 75% to >85% by end of Q2 by auditing the top 20 most-visited articles first, incorporating PM feedback, and planning a phased review of the remaining articles.
- By FY26 Q3, to address CSS feedback, publish 5 updated best practice guides for customers that promote adoption of high-margin services, and track engagement through internal feedback and usage metrics.
- In FY26Q3, address top 3 networking security opportunities in the docs using AI tools to fast-track deep analysis of CSS feedback and make recommendations so more time is spent on the fixes than the analysis. Validate opportunities with networking supportability lead.
- By end of FY26Q2, connect 100% of AI Foundry code templates to their corresponding Azure Architecture Center (AAC) guidance on Microsoft Learn and achieve a ≥20% increase in unique Learn visitors to the associated pages compared to the prior baseline.
- *(Manager example)* In Q4, sponsor a round of doc bashes in collaboration with Engineering, enabling directs to review and update the top 10% of unfresh articles in a highly strategic product area — measured by a 2% increase in quality score reported in the Content Quality Report.

---

## Goal 2: Continuously improve and innovate

We continuously improve how we work to better meet our customers' needs and drive business impact. We drive measurable improvements to our operations and standards, pilot bold experiments to solve our business problems, and scale what works for greater results. We reflect on what we have learned, share learnings with each other, and partner whenever and wherever possible.

### SMART goal examples

- By April, create and deliver 3 technical demos about docs contributions to internal product team audiences to increase content contributions from PMs by 25% and reduce low-value work item requests to writers — thereby improving PM self-sufficiency and enabling development of the next-period content strategy.
- By January, use AI to complete a full gap analysis of content against the customer JTA, auto-generate prioritized work items, and resolve the top 10 gaps by recruiting PMs to cover new feature content — freeing capacity to focus on filling gaps and other content strategy work.
- During the next period, achieve 100% adoption of GitHub Copilot for initial drafts and Documentor for secondary tasks (metadata, formatting, editing) across all content deliverables to improve velocity and quality — reserving human effort for high-value editorial work.
- By [month], improve operational efficiency by identifying the team's top three most time-consuming operational processes and deliver a version 1 automated solution for at least one — measured by reduced manual effort, improved efficiency, and documented feedback from impacted team members.
- By [month], improve AI readiness of the content area by aligning 150+ top-viewed service reference articles to approved patterns and ensuring full technical accuracy using AI and/or automation that references Learn standards and product source code.
- By [month], reduce time spent creating content for new service features by more than 25% by testing automation approaches and implementing the most effective solution.
- *(Manager example)* By end of period, identify and prioritize the team's top three most time-consuming operational processes and deliver version 1 automated solutions for each — measured by reduced manual effort, improved efficiency, and documented feedback from impacted team members.

---

## Goal 3: Work better together with peers and partners

We collaborate in measurable and meaningful ways with our peers, partners, and stakeholders in support of Learn Content's focus areas. When working towards our team goals, we act with urgency, join in with others, adapt, and pivot quickly. We connect with each other, step in to help fill gaps, and share our knowledge in ways that can lead to impact for others and foster a "better together" culture.

### SMART goal examples

- In [month], lead a Give Campaign event with colleagues to raise $2,000+ for a community cause. Success measured by participation rate in the org, the total amount raised, and stakeholder feedback highlighting leadership contribution.
- In [quarter], improve work planning habits across the M2 team by designing and delivering a required Azure DevOps workshop. Effectiveness measured by a reduction in recurring ADO issues, improved hygiene, and post-training feedback indicating improved planning practices.
- By [month], establish and facilitate a monthly peer feedback series focused on evaluating content impact and customer alignment. Success measured by participation from at least two writers per session and a 2% increase in customer engagement/satisfaction across articles over a six-month period.
- Throughout the period, host monthly AI community calls to provide a peer-learning forum. Track impact by identifying at least two cases per quarter where participation directly unblocked or enabled innovation.
- By [month], resolve a strained partnership with a stakeholder team by engaging senior leaders to align on vision, establishing quarterly business reviews with actionable insights, and adjusting support levels accordingly.
- By end of [quarter], increase PM participation in monthly content syncs from the current baseline to over 75% by conducting interviews to identify blockers and implementing targeted actions to address them.
- In [quarter], step in to provide interim coverage during a colleague's extended leave for a service area by balancing delivery requirements, meeting with partners regularly, and setting clear expectations with partner teams to maintain continuity and meet publishing goals.
- *(Manager example)* By [month], implement quarterly activities — including career conversations, mentoring sessions, and curated resource sharing — such that >75% of writers participate in each activity and show a 20% increase in perceived clarity of growth pathways, as captured in a post-initiative survey.
- *(Manager example)* By [month], ensure team AI fluency with core tooling by engaging 100% of members in active usage of GitHub Copilot and Documentor, culminating in a best practices summary.
